blueprint:
FINAL BLUEPRINT â€” "Insert what is sharp, size what is smooth, measure in mm first" (Angle A's skeleton, Angle B's mechanism-first ordering, both critiques' amendments folded in as binding).

VERDICT. Neither design ships as written. Angle B is rejected as a complete answer to defect 1 by its own admission: on any crest that is not a real mesh edge, refinement reaches amplitude < 0.05mm density-DEPENDENT â€” exactly the "finer but still present" outcome the user already rejected, and the literature (Kobbelt EMC: "the normal vectors do not converge") says no density ever closes it. Angle A's thesis (born-crest constraint insertion is the only density-independent cure) is correct and the machinery is production-proven, but Angle A as written has two disqualifying gaps found by the critiques and verified in this session against HEAD: (1) its Stage 1 is stale â€” the epsPosMm fix is COMMITTED at HEAD 88c40c1 (resolveSurfaceErrorMm at ParametricExportComputer.ts:2383, tests at QualityProfiles.test.ts:148-158), and (2) its 0.01-0.02mm amplitude gates are numerically unachievable against the measured SNAP FLOOR: inserted crest vertices are displaced by snapToCellEdge + snapToAnchor up to ~2.5x cornerSnap (FeatureConformingTriangulator.test.ts:323-326), with cornerSnap = 0.06/2^featureLevel = 4.7e-4 in u at featureLevel 7 (ConformingWall.ts:539) â€” ~0.2mm-class lateral physical displacement at r~70mm, density-independent, and itself an unexamined candidate for the very serration under investigation (SFB's 12 ALREADY-inserted crests carry this wobble today).

SYNTHESIS â€” the five governing decisions:

(1) CREST SERRATION needs BOTH mechanisms, ordered by evidence. Both styles the user can see (SFB@1, SpiralRidges) already have their crests pinned/inserted, and serration persists â€” so the most probable visible cause is the CROSS-crest band-quality mechanism the repo's own handoff names as the next fix: the dead earClip/efg max-min-angle diagonal path (QuadtreeTriangulator.ts:100-191 fires only when leaf.efg is set; PeriodicBalancedQuadtree never populates efg in production). Therefore the first production change after the instruments is efg/earClip activation (handoff Steps A/B/C), which simultaneously attacks the real committed baseline: 9/20 styles slivered at default (ArtDeco 2107, Crystalline 2027, DragonScales 2683, SpiralRidges 479, Voronoi 2076 + nonMan=1, ...), introduced by the GATE-B re-baseline. Born-crest insertion (Angle A Stage 3) runs immediately after â€” it is the only density-independent kill for the lateral staircase on the born crests and the 7 extractor-less styles â€” but it is re-adjudicated by the new instrument with an explicit kill criterion, because the repo twice measured insertion as not moving the (later-discredited) serration number.

(2) THE SNAP FLOOR is promoted to a first-class work item. Before any amplitude gate is frozen: audit the worst-case lateral mm displacement of inserted crest vertices (extraction grid error at 768x320 + cornerSnap + anchor snap). The placement fix that preserves BOTH watertightness and exactness: where a crest crosses a registry grid line, the crossing vertex is the analytic crest-x-grid-line INTERSECTION computed edge-locally (this is exactly what the registry contract already requires â€” same segment x same line => bit-identical point from both cells), and where snapToCellEdge/snapToAnchor would displace a crest vertex, slide it ALONG the analytic crest to that intersection instead of projecting perpendicular onto the edge â€” vertices stay exact analytic evaluations ON the ridge AND on the registry line, so the needle-triangle protection cornerSnap exists for is preserved by the same spacing discipline (TriWild sanitization + protecting-ball spacing tied to local quadtree level). If the audit shows today's wobble on the 12 inserted SFB crests is itself visible serration, this fix alone may move the user's render before any new insertion lands.

(3) CRYSTALLINE gets both levers, with the critique's parameter-space guards. The efg-guard hypothesis is REFUTED (96/376,351 leaves = 0.03%, fallback control measurably worse) â€” the guard is left alone. The measured three-part cause: (a) the 12 main grooves are true C1 creases (26.9 deg) on one constant-slope helical family u(t)=m/24-0.8t/24 â€” pinned via the EXISTING chooseHelixGrid + CreaseHelixWarp (production-proven on SpiralRidges; post-triangulation bijective shear, no connectivity change), replacing the measured-false '() => []' at FeatureLineGraph.ts:780; guards: derive k/turns from live params, explicit ridgePhase=0, refuse non-integer subFacets (fall back to today's behavior), route heightPhase=0/turns=0 to the CreaseUWarp vertical family (chooseHelixGrid refuses turns=0 by design); the dihedral gate applies to the 12 main apex creases only â€” the 24 sub-lines are tangent-continuous curvature blow-ups, a sizing problem. (b) The ~1350x kappa under-read (true 116.3/mm reads 0.086/mm; 78% of sizing nodes at the 1mm maxEdge clamp): thread the already-built-and-tested-but-dormant curvatureFloor/maxKappa (MetricSizingField.ts:49/:96-98, never passed at ConformingWall.ts:250) using the GENERIC route â€” tiny-step f64 FD on the existing CPU radius mirrors in geometry/styles.ts (all 20 styles) â€” not per-style derivations or the 16x GPU probe. (c) The chord-only error law: add h = min(sqrt(8*maxSag/kappa), thetaMax/kappa, maxEdge) with per-profile thetaMax RECALIBRATED against budget (high ~8-10 deg, ultra ~4 deg; epsNormalDeg=4 at minEdge=0.1mm is unreachable where kappa >= 0.7/mm â€” the gate is set at the floor-implied bound, not the wish). All Crystalline gates measured WITH searchBudgetScale binding (the governor uniformly coarsens away the new law otherwise).

(4) TOLERANCE GATES â€” mostly done, finish honestly. Remaining at HEAD: qMaxLevel still profile-name-driven (ParametricExportComputer.ts:2386-2388 â€” a tight override on draft/standard gets depth-clamped); epsFeatureMm has ZERO conforming-path consumers (verified: only legacy MeshValidator.ts:1904/2027 + the dialog) â€” wire it as an ADDITIONAL-ONLY tightener at the decimation candidate gate (featureDrop>0 stays an unconditional reject; epsFeatureMm may only tighten, never replace) or hide the slider on the conforming path; the quick exportSTL path (useParametricExport.ts:519-522) forwards only qualityProfile and drops toleranceOverrides â€” fix. Differential e2e so the slider can never go dead again, with chord conformance against the DECOUPLED reference (__pfReferenceDenseRes/bicubic â€” never the 256-sampler-shared reference) and the triangle-count-spread assertion made robust to maxEdge-clamp-dominated sizing (assert effectiveMaxSagMm propagation as the primary signal).

(5) MEASUREMENT DISCIPLINE is the spine. Every gate is in mm, absolute counts, and worst-case â€” never percent. Benchmark configs are PINNED (SFB at sf_strength=1 â€” default strength 0 extracts nothing per SF_CREST_MIN_STRENGTH=1e-3 at FeatureLineGraph.ts:680/:715 â€” ideally the exact configs of the user's renders). The REAL baseline is banked first (the '20/20 sliver-free' premise is stale; 9 styles carry slivers at default), so every later 'no regression / byte-identical' gate is falsifiable. Instrument calibration items are MEASUREMENTS that inform stages, not pass conditions (the critique's fix to Angle A's Stage-2(b) trap). Stage order maximizes early signal: instruments -> efg/earClip (the proven mechanism, also fixes the sliver baseline) in parallel with the snap audit -> born-crest insertion with exact placement -> Crystalline -> gated rollout -> CI lock-in.

WATERTIGHT ARGUMENT (absolute, per the rule): every stage is one of five proven-safe classes â€” (i) metric-only/read-only (instruments; export hash-identical), (ii) scalar sizing inputs (which cells exist; the registry + 2:1 balance handle any level mix by construction), (iii) cell-INTERIOR-only triangulation policy (earClip diagonal choice and any constrained-fill replacement consume the exact PASS-B edge-vertex union INCLUDING collinear points â€” proven with the existing two-cell wallEdgeAudit TDD pattern BEFORE any e2e; boundary sequences and constraint edges hard-immutable), (iv) registry-mediated constraint insertion (every crest-x-boundary crossing computed edge-locally and bit-identically by both cells; endpoints measured per style to terminate at seam/rim/clip-boundary â€” the SuperformulaBornCrests.test.ts pattern â€” with fold-point continuation-to-grid-line as mitigation and per-style deferral as fallback; clipFeaturesToBox keeps lines off the pinned t=0/1 cap rings), or (v) post-triangulation bijective domain warp (connectivity untouched; SpiralRidges precedent). B stays capped at 2 (WatertightAssembly.ts:156-161) until SFB@1 at B=3 measures bnd=nonMan=0; the cap comment already says 'Lifted by Stage 3's gate' â€” that gate is honored, never skipped. No post-hoc repair or weld anywhere. Cell-interior polyline endpoints (the one structurally untested FCT case) are measured per style before any insertion ships.

DEFINITION-OF-DONE MAPPING: SFB sharp ridges = clean dihedral, amplitude ~0 density-independent via insertion + exact along-crest placement (Stages 3-4); the visible band tail killed by efg/earClip + metric-aware fill (Stage 3); Crystalline grooves pinned density-independently + flank faceting bounded by the normal-error law (Stage 5); sliders verifiably change the file (Stage 1); all proven by the faithful instrument at high AND ultra (Stage 2), locked by CI (Stage 7). Honest statement required by the rules: refinement alone provably CANNOT reach amplitude ~0 on a non-edge crest â€” born-crest insertion is the answer, and its endpoint handling is specified above; if the Stage-3/4 kill criterion fires (amplitude does not drop after insertion with exact placement), the recorded fallback is that the visible sawtooth is band-quality/snap-wobble, and effort concentrates there with the instrument's component split as the arbiter.
faithfulMetricSpec:
crestLateralDeviation() in src/fidelity/metrics.ts + diagnoseCrestLateralDeviation in src/fidelity/windowHook.ts (clone of diagnoseCrestQuality at :646-664). Zero production-geometry code; export bytes hash-identical before/after (the 407a091 standard).

(1) TRUE RIDGE (reference error ~0 by construction): for SuperformulaBlossom use the CLOSED-FORM loci u*(t) = 4k*pi / (m(t)*2*pi) per petal k (the cos-power family), cross-checked against the exported sfRf (FeatureLineGraph.ts:702); for other styles, the exact root of g(u,t) = dr/du = 0 by bisection on the f64 CPU radius mirror (geometry/styles.ts) to |du| <= 1e-6 (<0.001mm physical), seeded by marchingSquaresZero at 768x320 and chained by predictor-corrector continuation along t; ridge births (fold points) located by solving g=0 AND dg/du=0 so branch endpoints are exact. EVERY amplitude number for a non-closed-form style is reported WITH its reference-error bound â€” an amplitude gate without the bound is meaningless.

(2) MESH CREST (the only genuinely new code, ~40 lines): z-plane slicer over the outer-wall submesh (extractOuterWallSubmesh + getLastConformingOuterWallMask, plumbing per windowHook.ts:611-644). Slice spacing = min(0.25mm, local cell t-extent). Per slice: intersect outer-wall triangles with the plane, take the local radius-maximum apex within a theta-window centered on theta_true(z); window = half the local crest spacing, with nearest-crest disambiguation near birth points by chaining along t from the rim following the continuation branch. Maxima and minima (crests vs valleys) kept separate. Slicer VALIDATED against a synthetic mesh with KNOWN deviation before any gate uses it; u-seam wrap and crest-birth chaining are explicit test cases.

(3) DEVIATION (the user's quantity â€” lateral, perpendicular to crest tangent, in mm): d = r * wrapPi(theta_mesh - theta_true) / sqrt(1 + (r * dtheta_true/dz)^2). Reported per crest as MAX and RMS amplitude; globally as the worst crest. DENSITY-INDEPENDENCE is the built-in discriminator: run at high AND ultra â€” a clean sharp edge reads ~0 (within reference error) at BOTH; a staircase reads ~half the local cross-crest cell and HALVES when density doubles. The pass condition for any fix is the constant-across-density reading, never the absolute number alone.

(4) SNAP/PLACEMENT AUDIT (separate channel, never conflated with mesh error): CPU-only measurement of the inserted-polyline-vs-analytic-ridge deviation â€” marching-squares extraction error + snapToCellEdge/snapToAnchor displacement (up to ~2.5x cornerSnap = 2.5 * 0.06/2^featureLevel in u, ~0.2mm-class physical at r~70mm). This number is published BEFORE the amplitude gates are frozen; the production gates are set at max(0.02mm, measured post-fix placement floor). Calibration items (e.g., 'born-crest band reads 0.05-0.3mm') are recorded as MEASUREMENTS informing the next stage, not pass/fail conditions.

(5) ABSOLUTE-COUNT COMPANION STATS (the anti-dilution requirement): surface bandBelow â€” already computed at metrics.ts:1797 but emitted only as a percent at :1825 â€” as an ABSOLUTE sub-15-degree triangle count, alongside the existing bandWorstMinAngleDeg (:1826), in crestBandTriangleQuality's result; plus per-style absolute sliver/nonMan/degenerate counts (the banked 9-style baseline) on every gate.

(6) CRYSTALLINE INSTRUMENT: groove-band dihedral histogram vs the analytic two-sided flank normals â€” ABSOLUTE edge counts above threshold and worst-case dihedral, with the SAME threshold for baseline and target (the >10 vs >20 deg mix-up is fixed); restricted to the 12 main apex creases; the 24 sub-lines get a separate curvature-band normal-jump metric (they are not dihedrals). Smooth-region worst-case + p99 dihedral vs the profile's floor-implied bound.

(7) PINNED CONFIGS: every gate names the exact style-parameter config (SFB at sf_strength=1 â€” default extracts nothing per SF_CREST_MIN_STRENGTH=1e-3; Crystalline at defaults; ideally the exact configs of the user's renders) and the exact profile. Reference levers: __pfReferenceDenseRes / __pfReferenceBicubic (windowHook.ts:624-636). No percent anywhere in any gate.

stagedPlan.stage: Stage 1 (STARTS NOW) â€” Tolerance gates completion + real baseline bank
stagedPlan.what: Re-scoped to HEAD 88c40c1 (epsPosMm fix is COMMITTED â€” do not redo). (a) Derive qMaxLevel from the RESOLVED sag instead of the profile name (ParametricExportComputer.ts:2386-2388). (b) epsFeatureMm: zero conforming consumers verified â€” either wire it as an ADDITIONAL-ONLY tightener at the decimation candidate gate (featureDrop>0 remains an unconditional reject) or hide the slider on the conforming path; no placebo ships. (c) Forward toleranceOverrides through the quick exportSTL path (useParametricExport.ts:519-522 drops them today). (d) Differential e2e: two epsPosMm values must produce distinct exports, chord conformance vs the DECOUPLED reference, count-spread assertion robust to maxEdge-clamp-dominated sizing (assert effectiveMaxSagMm propagation as the primary signal). (e) BANK THE BASELINE: per-style ABSOLUTE sliver/nonMan/degenerate/sub-15-deg counts for all 20 styles at default + pinned benchmark configs (SFB@1 etc.) â€” the committed record shows 9 styles slivered, so every later no-regression gate references this bank, not the stale '20/20 sliver-free'.
stagedPlan.gate: epsPosMm 0.10/0.05/0.02 on SFB@1 high: monotone-distinct triangle counts AND wallChordError p99 <= setting vs the decoupled reference (cusp band excluded); default-slider export byte-identical to HEAD; epsFeatureMm verifiably consumed or verifiably absent; quick-path export honors overrides; 20/20 topology bnd=nonMan=orient=0; baseline bank committed as an artifact.

stagedPlan.stage: Stage 2 â€” Faithful instrument: serration amplitude in mm + snap audit + absolute counts
stagedPlan.what: Build crestLateralDeviation + the z-plane slicer + the snap/placement audit + surfaced bandBelow/bandWorstMinAngleDeg + the Crystalline dihedral instrument, per faithfulMetricSpec. Calibration readings (inserted-crest amplitude, born-crest staircase, snap floor) recorded as measurements that SET the later gates â€” explicitly not pass/fail conditions. Production byte-identical (hash-verified).
stagedPlan.gate: Slicer validated against a synthetic mesh with known deviation; density-discrimination demonstrated on SFB@1 (high vs ultra readings published per crest with reference-error bounds); snap-floor number published (expected ~0.05-0.2mm class); export hashes unchanged; all stats absolute/worst-case/mm.

stagedPlan.stage: Stage 3 â€” Cross-crest quality: activate the dead efg/earClip path (handoff Steps A/B/C) â€” the proven mechanism and the sliver-baseline fix
stagedPlan.what: Step A: populate leaf.efg from the plain sampler in PeriodicBalancedQuadtree.leafOfCell/leaves() (firstFundamentalForm at :811 exists), activating earClipMaxMinAngle/shapedTemplate (QuadtreeTriangulator.ts:100-191) for all uBias>0 plain-path styles â€” targets the ArtDeco/Crystalline/DragonScales/GeometricStar/BasketWeave sliver classes. Step B: thread the WARP-COMPOSED efg for SpiralRidges (helixChoice.warp -> assembleWatertight -> buildConformingWall -> quadtree; sizing stays on the plain sampler). Step C: port the metric-aware diagonal choice into FeatureConformingTriangulator's plain-template branch (:661-681) and add a cell-interior-only improvement to the ConstrainedCellTriangulator fill â€” HARD REQUIREMENT: any replacement fill consumes the exact PASS-B edge-vertex union INCLUDING collinear edge points, proven with the two-cell wallEdgeAudit TDD pattern before any e2e; boundary/constraint edges immutable. EFG_MAX_REL_VARIATION guard left alone (refuted as the Crystalline cause; its subdivide-variant is deferred â€” ill-posed at C1 creases without a scale-convergence test). B stays capped at 2.
stagedPlan.gate: Banked-baseline comparison, all absolute: 9-style sliver counts driven to 0-or-near (SpiralRidges 479 -> ~0 via Step B; Voronoi nonMan 1 -> 0); SFB@1 crest-band sub-15-deg ABSOLUTE count reduced >=10x from the banked number with bandWorstMinAngleDeg >= 20 deg; LowPolyFacet/GothicArches absolute band counts <= current (the guard's epoch-1 regression must not return); 20/20 topology zeros; zoomed-render A/B vs the user's screenshots; full-20 re-baseline justified per style.
stagedPlan.effort: 3-4 days (Step C is the load-bearing unknown)

stagedPlan.stage: Stage 4 â€” Born-crest insertion with EXACT placement (the density-independent serration kill), re-adjudicated with a kill criterion
stagedPlan.what: (a) Placement fix first: crest-x-grid-line crossing vertices computed edge-locally as analytic intersections; crest vertices that would be snapped slide ALONG the analytic crest to the intersection (never perpendicular projection); tighten/eliminate the residual cornerSnap wobble for crest polylines with TriWild-style sanitization (Douglas-Peucker to 0.01mm 3D lateral tolerance, saliency threshold, near-duplicate merge) and protecting-ball spacing tied to local quadtree level. Watertightness re-proven via the registry contract + topology harness. Apply to the 12 ALREADY-inserted SFB crests too â€” the audit may show today's wobble is itself visible. (b) Un-defer SFB born crests: SF_CREST_FULL_HEIGHT_SPAN 0.85 -> ~0.05 (FeatureLineGraph.ts:685), keeping the strength>1e-3 gate; harden extraction to the closed-form loci with explicit fold-point endpoints landing on seam/rim grid lines (measured rim<->seam per SuperformulaBornCrests.test.ts; clipFeaturesToBox keeps lines off the pinned cap rings). (c) Timeboxed B=3 nonMan root cause (dedupSide/weld at high anisotropy, FeatureConformingTriangulator.ts:713-734); cap stays until SFB@1 B=3 measures bnd=nonMan=0.
stagedPlan.gate: SFB@1 born-crest lateral amplitude <= max(0.02mm, post-fix placement floor) at high AND ultra (density-INDEPENDENCE is the pass condition); featDrop=0 with featExp accounting; 20/20 topology zeros; other styles byte-identical. KILL CRITERION: if amplitude does not drop below the staircase baseline, the lateral staircase was not the visible sawtooth â€” halt the Stage-6 rollout, concentrate on Stage-3 residuals, record the component split.

stagedPlan.stage: Stage 5 â€” Crystalline: helix-crease pinning + sizing-law completion (both levers, guarded)
stagedPlan.what: LEVER 1: replace the measured-false '() => []' (FeatureLineGraph.ts:780) with the helical-crease family (k=24, turns=0.8, explicit ridgePhase=0) via existing chooseHelixGrid + CreaseHelixWarp; guards â€” derive k/turns from live params, refuse non-integer subFacets (today's behavior), route turns=0/heightPhase edge cases to the CreaseUWarp vertical family; fix the false 'smooth sum of sin/cos' doc (:53-57); verify warp-composed efg (Stage 3 Step B plumbing) reaches this new warp consumer; measure the phi0 column-pinning distortion (24 anchors among 32 columns). LEVER 2: thread curvatureFloor/maxKappa (built+tested, dormant â€” MetricSizingField.ts:49/:96-98, never passed at ConformingWall.ts:250) via the GENERIC f64-FD route on geometry/styles.ts CPU mirrors; add the normal-error term h=min(sqrt(8*maxSag/kappa), thetaMax/kappa, maxEdge) with per-profile thetaMax recalibrated against budget (high ~8-10 deg, ultra ~4 deg). DO NOT touch the efg guard or maxLevel (both refuted).
stagedPlan.gate: Crystalline@default high, all absolute and threshold-consistent: 12-main-crease band edges above the SAME dihedral threshold reduced >=10x with worst-case <15 deg AND counts STABLE high->ultra (density-independence); sub-line curvature-band metric improved (separate gate); smooth-region worst dihedral <= the floor-implied bound (~5.5 deg at minEdge 0.1mm/kappa 0.7) with p99 reported; triangle count <=2x current within the 6M budget, measured WITH searchBudgetScale binding; SpiralRidges byte-identical or strictly improved (shared machinery); absolute sliver counts not regressed (post-warp shear-sliver class watched explicitly); 20/20 topology zeros.

stagedPlan.stage: Stage 6 â€” Metric-gated extractor rollout to the remaining smooth-ridge styles
stagedPlan.what: One style at a time (HarmonicRipple, SuperellipseMorph, FourierBloom, WaveInterference, RippleInterference, ArtDeco), ONLY where Stage-2 shows staircase-dominant (density-halving) amplitude above the gate â€” honest-empty stays byte-identical (the strength-gate precedent). Per style: f64 CPU mirror, marchingSquaresZero on dr/du + continuation + fold-point endpoints, TriWild sanitization, exact along-crest placement from Stage 4(a), emitted as general-curve FeatureLines. ENDPOINT geometry MEASURED per style before insertion (the structurally untested cell-interior-endpoint case): seam/rim/clip-boundary termination or fold-point continuation extended to a registry grid line; otherwise that style defers. Sharp-but-rounded cusps (n<1): crest vertex at the intersection of the two analytic flank tangents (the EMC two-sided trick).
stagedPlan.gate: Per style before merge: lateral amplitude <= the Stage-4 gate at high AND ultra; topology zeros; absolute band sub-15-deg and sliver counts not regressed vs the bank; triangle count within profile budget; zero-extraction styles byte-identical; full-20 matrix re-run after EACH style lands.
stagedPlan.effort: 3-5 days total (0.5-1/style; some measure clean and need zero code)

stagedPlan.stage: Stage 7 â€” CI lock-in on real exports
stagedPlan.what: Sharpened claim: a no-GPU goal-vector gate exists; the missing gate is a REAL-GPU export gate. Add to the existing Playwright/WebGPU export-fidelity harness: SFB@1 + Crystalline + one smooth control at high, asserting serration amplitude, absolute band/sliver counts, groove dihedral counts, topology zeros, AND the epsPosMm differential (two settings -> different counts) so the slider can never silently die again. Runner feasibility (GPU-enabled CI) confirmed first; thresholds from the measured Stage 3-6 results.
stagedPlan.gate: CI red iff: amplitude above gate on any gated crest at either density; any absolute count above its banked threshold; any topology nonzero; or the differential produces identical counts. Green run banked as the new baseline.

quickWins: qMaxLevel from the RESOLVED sag instead of the profile name (ParametricExportComputer.ts:2386-2388) â€” one expression; a tight epsPosMm override on draft/standard is currently depth-clamped silently.
Forward toleranceOverrides through the quick exportSTL path (useParametricExport.ts:519-522 currently forwards only qualityProfile) â€” the slider is dead on that route even after 88c40c1.

epsFeatureMm placebo resolution: zero conforming-path consumers (verified) â€” hide/disable the feature-drift slider when the conforming path is active (ExportDialog.tsx:438-446) until a real consumer lands; shipping a dead control is a bug.

Surface bandBelow as an ABSOLUTE count (computed at metrics.ts:1797, emitted only as a percent at :1825) + bandWorstMinAngleDeg in crestBandTriangleQuality's public result and diagnoseCrestQuality â€” a few lines, immediately gives the user's anti-dilution number.

Bank the real 20-style baseline artifact (absolute sliver/nonMan/degenerate/sub-15-deg counts + pinned configs incl. SFB@sf_strength=1) â€” pure measurement, makes every later gate falsifiable; the '20/20 sliver-free' premise is stale (9 styles slivered at default).
Fix the factually false FeatureLineGraph doc comment (:53-57, 'smooth sum of sin/cos') that misdocuments Crystalline as crease-free â€” prevents the next session repeating the wrong bet.

Snap-displacement audit (CPU-only): publish the worst-case lateral mm wobble of inserted crest vertices (cornerSnap=0.06/2^featureLevel, up to ~2.5x per FeatureConformingTriangulator.test.ts:326) â€” sets every amplitude gate and may identify a visible serration source on the ALREADY-inserted SFB crests with zero production change.

rejected.idea: Angle B as the complete answer to crest serration (anisotropic/directed refinement + footprints, no insertion)
rejected.why: Mathematically cannot meet the DoD: on a crest that is not a mesh edge, refinement reaches amplitude ~half the local cross-crest spacing â€” density-DEPENDENT, halving per doubling â€” which is exactly the 'finer but still present' outcome the user already rejected with zoomed renders. Kobbelt et al. state it outright ('the normal vectors do not converge'), and Angle B's own coverage section concedes it. Its mechanism-first ordering, sizing-law work, and helix-warp stage are ADOPTED; the angle is rejected only as a complete cure for defect 1.

rejected.idea: Raising density / profile maxLevel as a serration or faceting cure
rejected.why: Measured no-op on the crest class (sampler 256->1024/2048 left worst min-angle byte-identical; the tree never passes L9 so maxLevel is dead headroom), and at 1.8M tris a 'small percent' is ~90k bad triangles â€” the user's dilution point. Density remains a chord-error tool only.

rejected.idea: refineCellInterior (isotropic Steiner insertion) in CDT feature cells
rejected.why: Measured HARMFUL on the curved surface (affine off-center back-map manufactures slivers: SFB@1 0->143, Hive 0->199, CelticKnot 0->99; ~2x build) and is the wrong tool â€” the residual slivers are anisotropic and isotropic insertion cannot square them. Stays behind __pfConformingRefine=false.

rejected.idea: Removing or loosening the EFG_MAX_REL_VARIATION guard as the Crystalline faceting fix
rejected.why: Hypothesis refuted by measurement: the guard trips on 96/376,351 leaves (0.03%), all far below visible scale, and the no-efg control build is measurably WORSE. The guard also exists because of a real epoch-1 regression (LowPolyFacet 0->7.8, GothicArches 0.7->4.4 sub-15-deg). Left alone.

rejected.idea: Subdivide-until-metric-constant as the unconditional efg-guard response (Angle B Stage 4)
rejected.why: Ill-posed at C1 creases: the FFF jump across a crease is scale-invariant, so a crease-straddling cell violates the 0.5 threshold at EVERY level â€” the predicate refines to the minEdge/maxLevel cap along every crease line on exactly the two epoch-1 regression styles, inflates the budget, then suppresses anyway; it also breaks the leafCount-only budget-search perf contract. Reconsider only with a scale-convergence test (subdivide only where variation DECREASES at child scale) and only if the per-style trip-rate table shows a material rate â€” deferred, not staged.

rejected.idea: epsFeatureMm REPLACING the binary featureDrop>0 decimation reject
rejected.why: Would LOOSEN the gate: a large slider value would ship dropped features. Drift and drop are different failure modes â€” epsFeatureMm may only be an additional tightener; featureDrop>0 stays an unconditional reject.

rejected.idea: Lifting the uBias B cap to 3 before the non-manifold root cause is fixed
rejected.why: SFB@1 at B=3 is measured genuinely non-manifold (3 duplicated total=4 edges) + sliver=2285, with no true-instrument gain over B=2 (band 40.6%->11.6% at B=2, crestRms within 4%). The cap (WatertightAssembly.ts:156-161) stays until bnd=nonMan=0 is measured at B=3; root-causing is timeboxed inside Stage 4(c).

rejected.idea: Any post-hoc repair/weld/T-junction-split pass to close insertion-induced cracks
rejected.why: Banned by the hard constraints and by project history â€” the legacy repair battery's proven defect factory (seam-zipper winding flips, O(bbox^3) T-junction grid hangs, center-fan needles) is why the conforming mesher exists. Watertightness is carried only by the edge-local grid-line registry contract, by construction.

rejected.idea: Freezing 0.01-0.02mm amplitude gates before the snap audit (Angle A as written)
rejected.why: Numerically unachievable against the verified snap floor: inserted crest vertices are displaced up to ~2.5x cornerSnap = 2.5*0.06/2^7 in u (~0.2mm-class physical at r~70mm), density-independent. Gates are set AFTER the audit at max(0.02mm, post-fix placement floor), and the placement fix (along-crest slide to the analytic crest-x-grid-line intersection) is a first-class stage item.

rejected.idea: Per-style analytic curvature derivations or the 16x denser GPU kappa probe as the default curvatureFloor route
rejected.why: superformulaAngularKappa exists for exactly one style, and __pfConformingDenseRes=1024 multiplies sampler cost ~16x. The generic route â€” tiny-step f64 finite differences on the existing CPU radius mirrors in geometry/styles.ts (all 20 styles already implemented) â€” is cheaper, uniform, and sufficient for a sizing floor. The others remain fallbacks if FD noise proves problematic.

rejected.idea: wallChordError against the 256-sampler-shared reference as a tolerance-conformance gate
rejected.why: The repo's own trap section disqualifies it: the reference shares the mesh's sampler and is blind to exactly the staircase class (the reference-domination failure that inflated crestRms ~2.6x and fooled a prior session). All chord gates use the decoupled __pfReferenceDenseRes/bicubic or analytic references.

openRisks: Stage 3 Step C (metric-aware constrained-cell fill) is the load-bearing unknown with no in-repo validation; inside a single constrained cell cdt2d leaves few flippable degrees of freedom, so the band residual may be vertex-PLACEMENT-limited â€” in which case the weight shifts to directed footprints/insertion and the gate's >=10x band reduction may need Stage 4's placement work to land first. Mitigation: wallEdgeAudit TDD before e2e; the kill-criterion data routes effort.

The snap/placement redesign (along-crest slide, tightened cornerSnap for crest polylines) touches the needle-triangle protection cornerSnap exists for; watertightness and sliver counts must be re-proven (registry contract + full topology harness), and a residual placement floor above 0.02mm would force the amplitude gates up â€” honest gates, but short of 'every triangle perfect'.

B=3 non-manifold root cause unresolved (suspect dedupSide/weld at high anisotropy); every new inserted feature family (born crests, Crystalline creases, Stage-6 styles) increases exposure to whatever coincidence breaks at deeper bias â€” full topology matrix re-run per stage, B capped at 2 throughout.

Triangle-budget blowout in Stage 5: the normal-error law demands ~80x local density where it binds (thetaMax 4 deg at kappa~1/mm), 78% of Crystalline sizing nodes sit at the maxEdge clamp today, and searchBudgetScale silently coarsens the law away when the budget binds â€” per-profile thetaMax recalibration + maxKappa + measuring with the governor binding are mandatory; the high profile's ~1M-tri feel may shift.

Cell-interior polyline endpoints remain the one structurally untested FeatureConformingTriangulator case; a Stage-6 style whose ridge births mid-surface away from seam/rim could expose it â€” per-style endpoint measurement is non-negotiable, deferral is the fallback.

Crystalline parameter space: non-integer subFacets (two incommensurate families one shear cannot pin), heightPhase/turns=0 (chooseHelixGrid refuses by design â€” must route to CreaseUWarp), edgeSharpness<1 (troughs become cusps the constant-slope guard does not see); the warp imports SpiralRidges' post-warp parallelogram-shear sliver class (mild at slope 1/30 but unmeasured) â€” guards must refuse to today's behavior, never silently mis-pin.

Metric reference error can dominate small amplitudes on non-closed-form styles; every amplitude number carries its reference-error bound or its gate is void â€” and the z-slicer's u-seam wrap / crest-birth chaining are correctness risks gated by the synthetic-mesh validation.

CI real-GPU runner feasibility is unverified (the existing gate is no-GPU goal-vector only); if no GPU runner exists, Stage 7 degrades to a pre-merge local ritual â€” weaker than the lock-in the cutover plan calls for.

Worktree hygiene: dozens of untracked probe artifacts and modified docs sit on refactor/core-migration, and tolerance-fix work has previously appeared mid-session from concurrent sessions â€” every stage commits its own isolated change with detect_changes() per project rules, nothing swept up.

Effort totals (~12-18 working days across 7 stages) assume no new defect classes surface from the 9-style sliver baseline fix in Stage 3; a full-20 re-baseline there could reveal interactions (e.g., earClip diagonals vs golden fixtures) that add re-measurement loops.
